  • Method
Package com.adrewdev.functional

Class Result<T,E>

java.lang.Object
com.adrewdev.functional.Result<T,E>
Type Parameters:
T - the type of the success value
E - the type of the error
public final class Result<T,E> extends Object
Represents a successful or failed operation.

Result is used to handle errors in a functional way, avoiding try/catch and allowing for composable error handling. It implements the Railway Pattern where operations can be chained and errors propagate automatically.

A Result is either:

  • Success(value) - contains a non-null success value
  • Failure(error) - contains a non-null error value

Example: 


 Result<User, String> result = Result.try_(
     () -> getUser(id),
     error -> "Failed to get user: " + error
 )
 .ensure(user -> user.isActive(), "User is not active")
 .bind(user -> Result.success(user.getEmail()));

 result.match(
     email -> System.out.println("Email: " + email),
     error -> System.out.println("Error: " + error)
 );
Author:
Ported from typescript-functional-extensions

    Nested Classes
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    static final class 
    Result.Pair<T,U>
    A simple immutable pair for holding two values.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    static <T, E> Result<List<T>,E>
    all(List<Result<T,E>> results)
    Combines a list of Results, returning all success values or the first failure.
    static <T, E> Result<T,E>
    any(List<Result<T,E>> results)
    Returns the first successful Result from a list, or the last failure if all fail.
    <U> Result<U,E>
    bind(Function<T,Result<U,E>> binder)
    Transforms the contained success value using the given function that returns a Result.
    <E2> Result<T,E2>
    bindError(Function<E,Result<T,E2>> binder)
    Transforms the error using a function that returns a Result.
    Maybe<E>
    compact()
    Converts a Result<Unit, E> to a Maybe for error handling.
    Result<T,E>
    ensure(Predicate<T> predicate, E error)
    Returns this Result if it contains a success value and the predicate matches, otherwise returns a Failure with the given error.
    boolean
    equals(Object obj)
    Compares this Result with another object for equality.
    static <T, E> Result<T,E>
    failure(E error)
    Creates a failed Result with the given error.
    static <T, E> Result<T,E>
    failureIf(boolean condition, E error, T value)
    Creates a failed Result if the condition is true.
    static <T, E> Result<T,E>
    failureIf(boolean condition, Supplier<E> errorSupplier, T value)
    Creates a failed Result if the condition is true, with lazy error evaluation.
    static <T, E> Result<T,E>
    first(Result<T,E>... results)
    Returns the first successful Result from varargs, or the first failure if all fail.
    static <T, E> Result<T,E>
    fromOptional(Optional<T> optional, E error)
    Creates a Result from an Optional, returning Failure with the given error if the Optional is empty.
    static <T, E> Result<T,E>
    fromOptional(Optional<T> optional, Supplier<E> errorSupplier)
    Creates a Result from an Optional, returning Failure with the error from the given supplier if the Optional is empty.
    E
    getErrorOrThrow()
    Returns the contained error value if present, throws NoSuchElementException otherwise.
    T
    getValueOrDefault(Supplier<T> defaultSupplier)
    Returns the success value if present, otherwise returns the value supplied by the given supplier.
    T
    getValueOrDefault(T defaultValue)
    Returns the success value if present, otherwise returns the given default value.

    getValueOrThrow()
    Returns the contained success value if present, throws NoSuchElementException otherwise.

    hashCode()
    Returns a hash code for this Result.

    Returns true if this Result represents a failed operation.
    boolean
    isSuccessful()
    Returns true if this Result represents a successful operation.

    Transforms the contained success value using the given function if present.

    mapError(Function<E,F> mapper)

    match(Function<T,R> onSuccess, Function<E,R> onFailure)

    merge(Result<U,E> other)

    static <T, E> Result<T,E>
    of(Supplier<Result<T,E>> supplier)
    Wraps a Result-returning supplier, catching any exceptions.
    Result<T,E>
    or(Result<T,E> other)
    Returns this Result if it contains a success value, otherwise returns the given Result.
    Result<T,E>
    or(Supplier<T> valueSupplier)
    Returns this Result if it contains a success value, otherwise returns a Result containing the value supplied by the given supplier.
    Result<T,E>
    or(T value)
    Returns this Result if it contains a success value, otherwise returns a Result containing the given value.
    Result<T,E>
    orElse(Result<T,E> other)
    Returns this Result if it contains a success value, otherwise returns the given Result.
    Result<T,E>
    orElseGet(Supplier<Result<T,E>> resultSupplier)
    Returns this Result if it contains a success value, otherwise returns a Result containing the value supplied by the given supplier.
    Result<T,E>
    recover(Function<E,T> recoverFn)
    Attempts to recover from a failure by applying a recovery function.

    success(T value)
    Creates a successful Result with the given value.
    static <T, E> Result<T,E>
    successIf(boolean condition, Supplier<T> valueSupplier, E error)
    Creates a successful Result if the condition is true, with lazy value evaluation.
    static <T, E> Result<T,E>
    successIf(boolean condition, T value, E error)
    Creates a successful Result if the condition is true, otherwise creates a failure.

    Executes the given consumer with the contained success value if present.

    Executes the given consumer with the contained error value if present.

    tapSuccess(Consumer<T> consumer)

  • Method Details

    • success

      public static <T, E> Result<T,E> success(T value)
      Creates a successful Result with the given value.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of the success value
      E - the type of the error
      Parameters:
      value - the success value (must not be null)
      Returns:
      a successful Result containing the value
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if value is null
      See Also:
      • failure(Object)
      • try_(Supplier, Function)

    • failure

      public static <T, E> Result<T,E> failure(E error)
      Creates a failed Result with the given error.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of the success value
      E - the type of the error
      Parameters:
      error - the error value (must not be null)
      Returns:
      a failed Result containing the error
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if error is null
      See Also:
      • success(Object)
      • try_(Supplier, Function)

    • try_

      public static <T, E> Result<T,E> try_(Supplier<T> supplier, Function<Throwable,E> errorHandler)
      Executes a supplier that may throw an exception, converting it to a Result.

      If the supplier succeeds, returns a successful Result. If it throws, returns a failed Result with the error from the handler.

      Example: 

      
 Result<Integer, String> result = Result.try_(
     () -> Integer.parseInt("42"),
     error -> "Parse failed: " + error.getMessage()
 );
 result.match(
     value -> System.out.println("Parsed: " + value),
     error -> System.out.println("Error: " + error)
 );
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of the success value
      E - the type of the error
      Parameters:
      supplier - the supplier to execute (must not be null)
      errorHandler - the function to convert exceptions to errors (must not be null)
      Returns:
      a Result containing the success value or error
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if supplier or errorHandler is null
      See Also:
      • success(Object)
      • failure(Object)
      • of(Supplier)

    • of

      public static <T, E> Result<T,E> of(Supplier<Result<T,E>> supplier)
      Wraps a Result-returning supplier, catching any exceptions.

      If the supplier throws an exception, returns a failed Result with the exception as the error.

      Example: 

      
 Result<Integer, String> result = Result.of(() -> {
     if (someCondition) {
         return Result.success(42);
     } else {
         return Result.failure("condition not met");
     }
 });
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of the success value
      E - the type of the error
      Parameters:
      supplier - the supplier to execute (must not be null)
      Returns:
      the Result from the supplier, or a failure if an exception occurred
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if supplier is null
      See Also:
      • try_(Supplier, Function)

    • isSuccessful

      public boolean isSuccessful()
      Returns true if this Result represents a successful operation.
      Returns:
      true if this is Success, false if this is Failure
      See Also:
      • isFailure()

    • isFailure

      public boolean isFailure()
      Returns true if this Result represents a failed operation.
      Returns:
      true if this is Failure, false if this is Success
      See Also:
      • isSuccessful()

    • getValueOrThrow

      public T getValueOrThrow()
      Returns the contained success value if present, throws NoSuchElementException otherwise.
      Returns:
      the contained success value
      Throws:
      NoSuchElementException - if this Result is a failure
      See Also:
      • getValueOrDefault(Object)
      • getErrorOrThrow()

    • getErrorOrThrow

      public E getErrorOrThrow()
      Returns the contained error value if present, throws NoSuchElementException otherwise.
      Returns:
      the contained error value
      Throws:
      NoSuchElementException - if this Result is successful
      See Also:
      • getValueOrThrow()

    • match

      public <R> R match(Function<T,R> onSuccess, Function<E,R> onFailure)
      Performs pattern matching on this Result.

      Calls onSuccess(value) if this is Success, or onFailure(error) if this is Failure.

      Type Parameters:
      R - the type of the result
      Parameters:
      onSuccess - the function to execute if this is Success (must not be null)
      onFailure - the function to execute if this is Failure (must not be null)
      Returns:
      the result of calling the appropriate callback
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if onSuccess or onFailure is null
      See Also:
      • match(Consumer, Consumer)

    • match

      public void match(Consumer<T> onSuccess, Consumer<E> onFailure)
      Performs pattern matching on this Result with void callbacks.

      Calls onSuccess.accept(value) if this is Success, or onFailure.accept(error) if this is Failure.

      Parameters:
      onSuccess - the consumer to execute with the value if this is Success (must not be null)
      onFailure - the consumer to execute with the error if this is Failure (must not be null)
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if onSuccess or onFailure is null
      See Also:
      • match(Function, Function)

    • map

      public <U> Result<U,E> map(Function<T,U> mapper)
      Transforms the contained success value using the given function if present.

      If this is Success, applies the mapper function to the value and wraps the result in a new successful Result. If this is Failure, returns the same Failure without calling the mapper.

      Type Parameters:
      U - the type of the transformed value
      Parameters:
      mapper - a function to apply to the value if present (must not be null)
      Returns:
      a Result containing the result of applying the mapper, or the same Failure if this is Failure
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if mapper is null
      See Also:
      • bind(Function)
      • tap(Consumer)

    • mapError

      public <F> Result<T,F> mapError(Function<E,F> mapper)
      Transforms the contained error value using the given function if present.

      If this is Failure, applies the mapper function to the error and wraps the result in a new failed Result. If this is Success, returns the same Success without calling the mapper.

      Type Parameters:
      F - the type of the transformed error
      Parameters:
      mapper - a function to apply to the error if present (must not be null)
      Returns:
      a Result containing the same success value, or a failed Result with the transformed error
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if mapper is null
      See Also:
      • map(Function)

    • bind

      public <U> Result<U,E> bind(Function<T,Result<U,E>> binder)
      Transforms the contained success value using the given function that returns a Result.

      Also known as "flatMap" or "chain". If this is Success, applies the binder function to the value and returns the resulting Result. If this is Failure, returns the same Failure without calling the binder.

      Type Parameters:
      U - the type of the transformed value
      Parameters:
      binder - a function to apply to the value if present, returning a Result (must not be null)
      Returns:
      the result of applying the binder, or the same Failure if this is Failure
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if binder is null
      See Also:
      • map(Function)
      • tap(Consumer)

    • tap

      public Result<T,E> tap(Consumer<T> consumer)
      Executes the given consumer with the contained success value if present.

      Useful for performing side effects on the value without transforming it. Returns this Result for method chaining.

      Parameters:
      consumer - a consumer to execute with the value if present (must not be null)
      Returns:
      this Result instance for chaining
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if consumer is null
      See Also:
      • map(Function)
      • bind(Function)

    • tapError

      public Result<T,E> tapError(Consumer<E> consumer)
      Executes the given consumer with the contained error value if present.

      Useful for performing side effects on the error without transforming it. Returns this Result for method chaining.

      Parameters:
      consumer - a consumer to execute with the error if present (must not be null)
      Returns:
      this Result instance for chaining
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if consumer is null
      See Also:
      • tap(Consumer)

    • ensure

      public Result<T,E> ensure(Predicate<T> predicate, E error)
      Returns this Result if it contains a success value and the predicate matches, otherwise returns a Failure with the given error.

      If this is Failure, returns the same Failure without evaluating the predicate.

      Parameters:
      predicate - a predicate to test the value against (must not be null)
      error - the error to return if the predicate returns false (must not be null)
      Returns:
      this Result if predicate matches, Failure(error) otherwise
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if predicate or error is null
      See Also:
      • where(Predicate, Function)

    • where

      public Result<T,E> where(Predicate<T> predicate, Function<T,E> errorFactory)
      Returns this Result if it contains a success value and the predicate matches, otherwise returns a Failure with the error from the given function.

      If this is Failure, returns the same Failure without evaluating the predicate.

      Parameters:
      predicate - a predicate to test the value against (must not be null)
      errorFactory - a function that creates the error if the predicate returns false (must not be null)
      Returns:
      this Result if predicate matches, Failure(errorFactory.apply(value)) otherwise
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if predicate or errorFactory is null
      See Also:
      • ensure(Predicate, Object)

    • or

      public Result<T,E> or(T value)
      Returns this Result if it contains a success value, otherwise returns a Result containing the given value.
      Parameters:
      value - the value to return if this is Failure
      Returns:
      this Result if Success, Success(value) if Failure
      See Also:
      • or(Supplier)
      • or(Result)

    • or

      public Result<T,E> or(Supplier<T> valueSupplier)
      Returns this Result if it contains a success value, otherwise returns a Result containing the value supplied by the given supplier.

      The supplier is only invoked if this is Failure.

      Parameters:
      valueSupplier - a supplier of the fallback value (must not be null)
      Returns:
      this Result if Success, Success(supplier.get()) if Failure
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if valueSupplier is null
      See Also:
      • or(Object)
      • or(Result)

    • or

      public Result<T,E> or(Result<T,E> other)
      Returns this Result if it contains a success value, otherwise returns the given Result.
      Parameters:
      other - the Result to return if this is Failure (must not be null)
      Returns:
      this Result if Success, other if Failure
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if other is null
      See Also:
      • orElse(Result)
      • or(Object)

    • orElse

      public Result<T,E> orElse(Result<T,E> other)
      Returns this Result if it contains a success value, otherwise returns the given Result.

      This is an alias for or(Result) and behaves identically.

      Parameters:
      other - the Result to return if this is Failure
      Returns:
      this Result if Success, other if Failure
      See Also:
      • or(Result)

    • orElseGet

      public Result<T,E> orElseGet(Supplier<Result<T,E>> resultSupplier)
      Returns this Result if it contains a success value, otherwise returns a Result containing the value supplied by the given supplier.

      The supplier is only invoked if this is Failure.

      Parameters:
      resultSupplier - a supplier of the fallback Result (must not be null)
      Returns:
      this Result if Success, supplier.get() if Failure
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if resultSupplier is null
      See Also:
      • or(Supplier)
      • or(Result)

    • getValueOrDefault

      public T getValueOrDefault(T defaultValue)
      Returns the success value if present, otherwise returns the given default value.
      Parameters:
      defaultValue - the value to return if this is Failure
      Returns:
      the success value if present, otherwise the default value
      See Also:
      • getValueOrDefault(Supplier)
      • getValueOrThrow()

    • getValueOrDefault

      public T getValueOrDefault(Supplier<T> defaultSupplier)
      Returns the success value if present, otherwise returns the value supplied by the given supplier.

      The supplier is only invoked if this is Failure.

      Parameters:
      defaultSupplier - a supplier of the default value (must not be null)
      Returns:
      the success value if present, otherwise the supplied default value
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if defaultSupplier is null
      See Also:
      • getValueOrDefault(Object)
      • getValueOrThrow()

    • toOptional

      public Optional<T> toOptional()
      Returns the underlying Optional containing the success value.

      If this Result is a Failure, returns an empty Optional.

      Returns:
      the Optional wrapping the success value
      See Also:
      • #fromOptional(Optional)

    • fromOptional

      public static <T, E> Result<T,E> fromOptional(Optional<T> optional, E error)
      Creates a Result from an Optional, returning Failure with the given error if the Optional is empty.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of the value
      E - the type of the error
      Parameters:
      optional - the Optional to convert (must not be null)
      error - the error to return if the Optional is empty (must not be null)
      Returns:
      Success(value) if the Optional contains a value, Failure(error) otherwise
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if optional or error is null
      See Also:
      • toOptional()

    • fromOptional

      public static <T, E> Result<T,E> fromOptional(Optional<T> optional, Supplier<E> errorSupplier)
      Creates a Result from an Optional, returning Failure with the error from the given supplier if the Optional is empty.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of the value
      E - the type of the error
      Parameters:
      optional - the Optional to convert (must not be null)
      errorSupplier - a supplier of the error to return if the Optional is empty (must not be null)
      Returns:
      Success(value) if the Optional contains a value, Failure(errorSupplier.get()) otherwise
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if optional or errorSupplier is null
      See Also:
      • toOptional()

    • equals

      public boolean equals(Object obj)
      Compares this Result with another object for equality.

      Two Results are equal if they are both Success with equal values, or both Failure with equal errors.

      Overrides:
      equals in class Object
      Parameters:
      obj - the object to compare with
      Returns:
      true if the Results are equal, false otherwise

    • hashCode

      public int hashCode()
      Returns a hash code for this Result.
      Overrides:
      hashCode in class Object
      Returns:
      the hash code based on success/error state and value

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns a string representation of this Result.
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      "Success{value}" if this is Success, "Failure{error}" if this is Failure

    • successIf

      public static <T, E> Result<T,E> successIf(boolean condition, T value, E error)
      Creates a successful Result if the condition is true, otherwise creates a failure.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of the success value
      E - the type of the error
      Parameters:
      condition - the condition to check
      value - the value to wrap if condition is true
      error - the error to use if condition is false
      Returns:
      a Result that is successful if condition is true
      See Also:
      • failureIf(boolean, Object, Object)

    • successIf

      public static <T, E> Result<T,E> successIf(boolean condition, Supplier<T> valueSupplier, E error)
      Creates a successful Result if the condition is true, with lazy value evaluation.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of the success value
      E - the type of the error
      Parameters:
      condition - the condition to check
      valueSupplier - the supplier of the value to wrap if condition is true (must not be null)
      error - the error to use if condition is false
      Returns:
      a Result that is successful if condition is true
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if valueSupplier is null
      See Also:
      • successIf(boolean, Object, Object)

    • failureIf

      public static <T, E> Result<T,E> failureIf(boolean condition, E error, T value)
      Creates a failed Result if the condition is true.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of the success value
      E - the type of the error
      Parameters:
      condition - the condition to check
      error - the error to use if condition is true
      value - the value to use if condition is false
      Returns:
      a Result that is failed if condition is true
      See Also:
      • successIf(boolean, Object, Object)

    • failureIf

      public static <T, E> Result<T,E> failureIf(boolean condition, Supplier<E> errorSupplier, T value)
      Creates a failed Result if the condition is true, with lazy error evaluation.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of the success value
      E - the type of the error
      Parameters:
      condition - the condition to check
      errorSupplier - the supplier of the error to use if condition is true (must not be null)
      value - the value to use if condition is false
      Returns:
      a Result that is failed if condition is true
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if errorSupplier is null
      See Also:
      • failureIf(boolean, Object, Object)

    • zip

      public <T2, R> Result<R,E> zip(Result<T2,E> other, BiFunction<T,T2,R> combiner)
      Combines this Result with another Result using the given combiner function.

      If both Results are successful, applies the combiner and returns success. If either is failed, returns the first failure.

      Type Parameters:
      T2 - the type of the other success value
      R - the type of the combined result
      Parameters:
      other - the other Result to combine with (must not be null)
      combiner - the function to combine the values (must not be null)
      Returns:
      a Result with the combined value, or the first failure
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if other or combiner is null
      See Also:
      • merge(Result)

    • merge

      public <U> Result<Result.Pair<T,U>,E> merge(Result<U,E> other)
      Merges two successful Results into a Pair, or returns the first failure.
      Type Parameters:
      U - the type of the other success value
      Parameters:
      other - the other Result to merge with (must not be null)
      Returns:
      a Result with a Pair of values, or the first failure
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if other is null
      See Also:
      • zip(Result, BiFunction)

    • all

      public static <T, E> Result<List<T>,E> all(List<Result<T,E>> results)
      Combines a list of Results, returning all success values or the first failure.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of the success values
      E - the type of the error
      Parameters:
      results - the list of Results to combine (must not be null)
      Returns:
      a Result with a list of all success values, or the first failure
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if results is null
      See Also:
      • any(List)
      • first(Result...)

    • any

      public static <T, E> Result<T,E> any(List<Result<T,E>> results)
      Returns the first successful Result from a list, or the last failure if all fail.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of the success value
      E - the type of the error
      Parameters:
      results - the list of Results to check (must not be null)
      Returns:
      the first successful Result, or the last failure if all fail
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if results is null
      See Also:
      • all(List)
      • first(Result...)

    • first

      @SafeVarargs public static <T, E> Result<T,E> first(Result<T,E>... results)
      Returns the first successful Result from varargs, or the first failure if all fail.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of the success value
      E - the type of the error
      Parameters:
      results - the varargs Results to check
      Returns:
      the first successful Result, or the first failure if all fail
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if results is null
      See Also:
      • all(List)
      • any(List)

    • recover

      public Result<T,E> recover(Function<E,T> recoverFn)
      Attempts to recover from a failure by applying a recovery function.

      If this Result is successful, returns it unchanged. If it is failed, applies the recovery function to the error and returns success.

      Parameters:
      recoverFn - the function to recover from error (must not be null)
      Returns:
      a successful Result with either the original value or recovered value
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if recoverFn is null
      See Also:
      • bindError(Function)

    • bindError

      public <E2> Result<T,E2> bindError(Function<E,Result<T,E2>> binder)
      Transforms the error using a function that returns a Result. Similar to bind but for errors.
      Type Parameters:
      E2 - the type of the new error
      Parameters:
      binder - the function to transform the error (must not be null)
      Returns:
      a Result with the transformed error, or the same success
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if binder is null
      See Also:
      • recover(Function)

    • tapSuccess

      public Result<T,E> tapSuccess(Consumer<T> consumer)
      Performs a side effect on the success value. Alias for tap().
      Parameters:
      consumer - the consumer to apply to the success value (must not be null)
      Returns:
      this Result (unchanged)
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if consumer is null
      See Also:
      • tap(Consumer)
      • tapFailure(Consumer)

    • tapFailure

      public Result<T,E> tapFailure(Consumer<E> consumer)
      Performs a side effect on the error value.
      Parameters:
      consumer - the consumer to apply to the error (must not be null)
      Returns:
      this Result (unchanged)
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if consumer is null
      See Also:
      • tap(Consumer)
      • tapSuccess(Consumer)

    • compact

      public Maybe<E> compact()
      Converts a Result<Unit, E> to a Maybe for error handling. Useful for integrating with Maybe-based error handling.
      Returns:
      a Maybe containing the error if this is a failure, or none if successful
      See Also:
      • Maybe.some(Object)
      • Maybe.none()
